info.title: Wokelo API
info.description:
Wokelo provides four categories of APIs for company and market intelligence:

1. **Enrichment** – Company and Industry data using Wokelo database and alternative data. Supports multi-company requests.
2. **Discovery** – Workflows for specific use-cases such as market map, target screening, and buyer screening.
3. **Monitoring** – Real-time news monitoring for companies.
4. **Workflow Automation** – Wokelo and custom research workflows executed within notebooks.

Enrichment and Discovery APIs are managed through async requests; use the Supporting APIs to track status and export results in JSON.
Workflow APIs are executed within notebooks and support export to PDF, PPT, DOC, or JSON.

**Authentication:** All endpoints (except `/auth/token/`) require a Bearer JWT token in the `Authorization` header.

bearerAuth.description: JWT token obtained from the `/auth/token/` endpoint.

prompt.example: Rate this company's AI readiness on a scale of 1-10 with reasoning

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Obtain JWT tokens for API access

tags.name: Enrichment
tags.description: Retrieve structured data for companies and industries

tags.name: Discovery
tags.description: Identify companies via market maps, buyer and target screening

tags.name: Workflow Automation
tags.description: Research notebooks for companies, industries, and custom workflows

tags.name: Supporting APIs
tags.description: Utility endpoints for search, file upload, request management, and report retrieval

post.description:
Obtain a JSON Web Token (JWT) for subsequent API authentication.
Submit credentials as `multipart/form-data`. The returned `access_token`
must be included as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in all subsequent requests.

client_id.description: Unique identifier for your organization, from the API Credentials page.

client_secret.description: Secret key for your organization, from the API Credentials page.

post.description:
Delivers structured data for a single company across sections such as firmographics, products, funding, headcount, and more.
Returns the enriched data directly in the response.

company.description: A permalink or URL of the company to enrich.

sections.description: Sections of data to include in the enrichment response. Pass all values to get full data.

data.description: Enriched company data keyed by section name.

post.description: Delivers / data for one or more companies. Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

companies.description: List of company permalinks or URLs to enrich.

sections.description: Sections of data to include in the enrichment response. Select one or more values.

parameters.description: Optional parameters to customize the output.

custom_fields.description: List of custom fields to compute for each company.

prompt.description: Prompt used to generate the custom field value.
prompt.example: Rate this company's AI readiness on a scale of 1-10 with reasoning

post.description: Generates structured insights for a given industry or sector. Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

topic.description: The industry topic to generate insights for.

sections.description: Sections to include in the report. Select one or more values.

parameters.description: Optional filter parameters.

keywords.description: Keywords to focus the analysis on.

geography.description: Geographic scope as ISO country codes.

definition.description: A custom definition of the industry to guide report generation.
definition.example: B2B software focused on enterprise cybersecurity

sample_companies.description: Permalinks of representative companies in the industry.

parameters.definition: B2B software focused on enterprise cybersecurity

get.description: Fetch G2 product reviews for a given company.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Company permalink or URL (e.g., `salesforce`)

get.description: Fetch SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, etc.) for a public company.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Company permalink or URL

get.description: Fetch Glassdoor employee reviews for a company.
Fetch earnings call transcripts for a public company.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Company permalink or URL

post.description:
Generates a long list of companies operating in a given market, enriched with structured data.
Supports filtering by geography, company type, employee count, funding stage, financial metrics, and more.
Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

parameters.description: Optional filter parameters object. Send empty object if no filters needed.

employee_count.description: Filter by employee headcount range. Pass an empty array to include all headcounts.

founded_year.description: Filter by company founding year. Accepts `from` and/or `to` integer values.

funding_stage.description: Filter by funding stage. Pass an empty array to include all stages.

total_funding.description: Filter by total funding amount raised. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

last_funding_round.description: Filter by last funding round amount. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

from.description: Minimum last funding round in USD

to.description: Maximum last funding round in USD

revenue.description: Filter by annual revenue. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

ebitda.description: Filter by EBITDA. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

net_income.description: Filter by net income. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

ev_ebitda.description: Filter by EV/EBITDA multiple. Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

post.description:
Generates a long list of companies similar to a given company, enriched with structured data.
Supports filtering by geography, company type, employee count, funding stage, financial metrics, and more.
Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

company.description: URL or permalink of the company whose peers need to be found.

parameters.description: Optional filter parameters. All fields must always be included in the request body — send empty values for any fields you do not want to filter on.

detailed_query.description: A more descriptive query to refine the search scope.

sample_companies.description: Permalinks of representative companies or their complete URLs.

geography.description: Geographic scope as ISO country codes (e.g. ["USA", "GBR"]). Pass an empty array for all geographies.

employee_count.description: Filter by employee headcount range. Pass an empty array to include all headcounts.

funding_stage.description: Filter by funding stage. Pass an empty array to include all stages.

total_funding.description: Filter by total funding amount raised (in USD).

last_funding_round.description: Filter by last funding round amount (in USD).

from.description: Minimum last funding round in USD

to.description: Maximum last funding round in USD

post.description:
Identifies potential acquirers for a target company based on strategic fit criteria.
Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

company.description: Permalink or URL of the target company being sold/acquired.

parameters.description: Optional filter parameters. All fields must always be included in the request body — send empty values for any fields you do not want to filter on.

detailed_query.description: A descriptive query to refine the buyer search scope and strategic criteria.
detailed_query.example: Looking for strategic acquirers in enterprise software

buyer_type.description: Type of buyer to target. Pass an empty array to include all buyer types.

geography.description: Geographic scope as ISO country codes (e.g. ["USA", "GBR"]). Pass an empty array for all geographies.

parameters.detailed_query: Looking for strategic acquirers in enterprise software

post.description:
Identifies potential acquisition targets for an acquirer company based on strategic criteria.
Returns a `request_id` for async polling.

company.description: Permalink or URL of the acquiring company.

parameters.description: Optional filter parameters. All fields must always be included in the request body — send empty values for any fields you do not want to filter on.

detailed_query.description: A descriptive query to refine the target search scope.
detailed_query.example: Looking for B2B SaaS companies in the cybersecurity space

keywords.description: Keywords used to identify and filter relevant target companies.

sample_companies.description: Permalinks of representative companies to anchor the target search.

geography.description: Geographic scope as ISO country codes (e.g. ["USA", "GBR"]). Pass an empty array for all geographies.

employee_count.description: Filter by employee headcount range. Pass an empty array to include all headcounts.

founded_year.description: Filter by company founding year. Accepts `from` and/or `to` integer values.

funding_stage.description: Filter by funding stage. Pass an empty array to include all stages.

total_funding.description: Filter by total funding amount raised (in USD). Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

revenue.description: Filter by annual revenue (in USD). Accepts `from` and/or `to` values.

parameters.detailed_query: Looking for B2B SaaS companies in the cybersecurity space

post.description: Fetches the latest news articles based on user-defined filters such as sentiment, geography, date range, and more. Results are returned directly in the response.

industry.description: List of industry topics for which news articles should be fetched.

blacklisted.description: List of publisher domains to exclude from results.

limit.description: Maximum number of news articles to return. Default: 100, Max: 1000.

offset.description: Number of articles to skip before starting to return results.

geo.description: List of 3-letter ISO country codes to filter by geography.

breaking_news.description: Set to `true` to fetch breaking news only.

get.description:
Fetches the latest news articles for a given company, enriched with AI-generated summaries,
categories, sentiment, and publisher details. Results are returned directly in the HTTP response
(no async polling needed).

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Company permalink or URL (e.g., `tesla` or `https://www.tesla.com/`).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of news categories to filter by.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for news fetch (e.g., `2024-01-01`).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for news fetch (e.g., `2024-12-31`).

parameters.name: publishers
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of publisher domains to include.

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of publisher domains to exclude.

get.description:
Fetches the latest news articles for a given industry topic, enriched with AI-generated summaries,
categories and publisher details. Results are returned directly in the HTTP response.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Industry topic for which news articles should be fetched (e.g., 'Warehouse Automation').

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of publisher domains to exclude from results.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for news fetch (e.g., `2024-01-01`).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for news fetch (e.g., `2024-12-31`).

parameters.name: geo
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of 3 letter ISO country codes.

post.description:
Initiates a full company research workflow inside a Wokelo notebook.
Use `/api/enterprise/report/status/` to track completion and
`/api/enterprise/report/download/` to export to PDF, PPT, DOC, or JSON.
Initiates a full industry research workflow inside a Wokelo notebook.
Initiates a peer/competitor comparison workflow for a set of companies inside a Wokelo notebook.
Runs a user-defined workflow within a Wokelo notebook using a custom prompt or template.

workflow_prompt.description: Custom prompt describing the research task.

post.description:
Starts generating a report for a given workflow type. Pass the appropriate `workflow` value to trigger Company Research, Industry Research, Peer Comparison, or a Custom Workflow. Returns a `report_id` to track and download the report.

**Workflow values:**
- `company_primer` – Company Research
- `industry_primer` – Industry Research
- `player_comparison` – Peer Comparison
- Your custom workflow ID (e.g. `comp_research_f312g`) – Custom Workflows

workflow.description: Workflow identifier. Use `company_primer`, `industry_primer`, `player_comparison`, or your custom workflow ID from the dashboard URL.

permalink.description: Crunchbase permalink of the company. Required for company-based workflows (either `permalink` or `website` must be provided).

website.description: Website of the company. Required for company-based workflows (either `permalink` or `website` must be provided).

websites.description: Array of company websites to compare. Required for `player_comparison` workflow.

industry.description: Company's industry. If not provided for company workflows, Wokelo will auto-detect it.

industry_research_topic.description: For industry workflows: the industry to analyze (required). For company workflows: the company's industry (optional).

workbook_name.description: Name with which the workbook will be saved.

custom_files.description: Files to include in the report. Use `fileName` values from the Upload File response.

200.description: Workflow started successfully. Returns the `report_id` to use with Get Report Status and Download Report.

report_id.description: Report ID for the initiated workflow report.

400.description: Bad request – missing or invalid parameters

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

get.description: Search for companies using name, website, or ticker. Returns a list of companies with their permalink and other attributes.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string (e.g., company name, website or ticker).

parameters.name: search_by
parameters.description: Field to search by. Supported values: `name`, `website`, `ticker`.

parameters.name: company_type
parameters.description: Filter by company type. Use `all` to return all types (default).

industries.description: Industry or sector the company operates in.

last_funding_round.description: Most recent funding round (e.g., Seed, Pre Seed).

data.name: Wokelo AI
data.short_description: Wokelo builds purpose-built AI agents for dealmaking and consulting.

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

post.description:
Upload files to Wokelo to supplement research reports with proprietary data. Each uploaded file receives a unique identifier (`fileName`) that can be referenced in the `custom_files` parameter when creating reports.

- **Individual uploads preferred** – for best performance, upload files one at a time.
- **Bulk upload limits** – maximum 10 files per request, 20 MB limit per file.

files.description: Array of files to be uploaded.

fileUUID.description: Your own UUIDs to track files. Must match the length and order of the `files` array. These UUIDs will be returned in the response alongside Wokelo's `fileName` identifiers.

200.description: Upload response including successfully uploaded files and any failures.

success.description: Array containing information on successfully uploaded files.

fileName.description: Wokelo's unique file identifier – use this in `custom_files` when creating reports.

uuid.description: Your provided UUID (if supplied in request).

fails.description: Array containing information about failed uploads (empty on full success).

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

get.description: Retrieves the current status of a previously submitted async API request.

post.description: Cancels a request already submitted. Pass the unique `request_id` to cancel a pending or in-progress async request.

request_id.description: The unique identifier of the request to be cancelled.

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

get.description: Retrieves the final JSON result of a COMPLETED async request.

Retrieves the current status of a previously triggered report using its unique ID. Poll this endpoint until the status is `Completed`, `Exported`, or `No Data Found`.

Possible status values:
- `Completed`
- `Processing`
- `Queued`
- `Delayed`
- `Exported`
- `No Data Found`

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.description: The ID returned in the response to the start report request.

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

post.description:
Downloads the generated report in the required format. Supported formats: `pdf`, `docx`, and `json`.

- For `json` format, the response body contains the structured report data.
- For `pdf` and `docx`, the response contains the binary file content.

report_id.description: ID of the report to be exported.

200.description: Report downloaded successfully. Returns binary file for `pdf`/`docx`, or structured JSON data for `json` format.

schema.description: Structured report data (returned only when `file_type` is `json`).

source.title: L4 Self-driving Vehicle Market Current Status and Future Prospects till 2033

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

get.description: Fetch earnings call transcripts for a public company. Supports year-range filtering via `from_year` and `to_year`.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Permalink or URL of the company.

parameters.name: from_year
parameters.description: Start year of the date range.

parameters.name: to_year
parameters.description: End year of the date range.

data.description: List of earnings call transcript objects.

transcript_title.description: Short title of the earnings call.

transcript_name.description: Full name of the earnings call including date.
transcript_name.example: Tesla, Inc., Q4 2023 Earnings Call, Jan 24, 2024

ticker.description: Stock ticker symbol with exchange prefix.

transcript_url.description: URL to the raw transcript file.

date.description: Date the earnings call took place.

year.description: Fiscal year of the earnings call.

quarter.description: Fiscal quarter of the earnings call (1–4).

participant.description: List of participants (executives and analysts) on the call.

transcript.description: Ordered list of speeches from the earnings call.

speech.description: Full text of the speech segment.

person_company.description: Company of the speaker, if available.

component_order.description: Order index of this speech within the transcript.

data.transcript_name: Tesla, Inc., Q4 2023 Earnings Call, Jan 24, 2024

participant.name: Elon Musk
participant.job_title: Co-Founder, Technoking of Tesla, CEO & Director

transcript.name: Martin Viecha
transcript.speech: Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to Tesla's Fourth Quarter 2023 Q&A Webcast.

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token

post.description: Retrieves and returns the settings originally configured while initiating the report. Pass the `notebook_id` (report ID) to get all initial configuration attributes used when triggering the workflow.

notebook_id.description: Report ID of the notebook for which the configuration is to be retrieved.

200.description: Notebook configuration retrieved successfully. Returns all initial configuration attributes used when the workflow was triggered.

schema.description: All initial configuration attributes added while triggering the workflow.

401.description: Unauthorized – invalid or missing JWT token
